children.name: ValidateAndPrepareSend
children.purpose: Validate that the user is a member of the target channel and prepare the message data for storage.
children.behavior: Check if user_id is in channel's member list; if not, return error. Otherwise, prepare message content with timestamp.
children.signature: def ValidateAndPrepareSend(user_id: str, channel_id: str, content: str, channels: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read channel member list for membership validation

data_operations.description: Read channel data to check if user is a member

constraints.description: Must not modify any data store during validation

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error dict if user is not a member of the channel
Returns success dict with prepared message (including timestamp) if user is a member

children.name: StoreMessage
children.purpose: Store the validated message in the messages data store.
children.behavior: Insert the message into the messages dict with a new message_id and current timestamp.
children.signature: def StoreMessage(prepared_message: dict, messages: dict) -> str

global_vars.description: Write new message entry to messages store

data_operations.description: Insert new message into messages database

constraints.description: Must generate a unique message_id for each insertion

acceptance_criteria.description: Message is successfully stored in the messages dict

children.name: UpdateUserLastSeen
children.purpose: Update the user's last_seen timestamp to current time.
children.behavior: Set the last_seen field of the user in the users store to current timestamp.
children.signature: def UpdateUserLastSeen(user_id: str, users: dict) -> bool

global_vars.description: Write updated last_seen timestamp for user

constraints.description: Must use current system timestamp for the update

acceptance_criteria.description: User's last_seen is updated to current timestamp

children.name: NotifyChannelMembers
children.purpose: Notify all other members of the channel about the new message.
children.behavior: Iterate over channel members excluding sender, and send a notification (simulated).
children.signature: def NotifyChannelMembers(channel_id: str, user_id: str, channels: dict) -> bool

global_vars.description: Read channel member list to determine notification recipients

data_operations.description: Read channel data to get member list for notification

constraints.description: Must not send notification to the sender (user_id)

acceptance_criteria.description: All channel members except sender receive notification
Returns True if notifications dispatched successfully

children.name: RetrieveMessageHistory
children.purpose: Retrieve the last 100 messages from a channel, sorted by timestamp descending.
children.behavior: Query messages store for channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100.
children.signature: def RetrieveMessageHistory(channel_id: str, messages: dict) -> list

global_vars.description: Read messages from messages store for history retrieval

constraints.description: Must sort messages by timestamp in descending order

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of messages for the specified channel
List is sorted by timestamp descending

children.name: CreateChannel
children.purpose: Create a new channel with unique name, set caller as creator and first member.
children.behavior: Check if channel name already exists; if not, create new channel entry with members list containing user_id.
children.signature: def CreateChannel(channel_name: str, user_id: str, channels: dict) -> str

global_vars.description: Read to check name uniqueness, write to create new channel

data_operations.description: Read existing channels to check name uniqueness

constraints.description: Channel name must be unique across all channels

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if channel name already exists
Creates new channel with creator as first member

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Add a user to a channel's member list if not already a member.
children.behavior: Check if user is already in channel's members; if not, add user_id to members list.
children.signature: def JoinChannel(channel_id: str, user_id: str, channels: dict) -> bool

global_vars.description: Read channel member list, write to add new member

data_operations.description: Read channel data to check current members
Write updated member list with new user

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns False if user is already a member
Adds user to channel members list

governance_notes: Parent global state conservation verified: messages (read_write) covered by StoreMessage (write) and RetrieveMessageHistory (read); users (read) covered by UpdateUserLastSeen (write) - note: parent requires read but children only need write for last_seen update; channels (read_write) covered by ValidateAndPrepareSend (read), NotifyChannelMembers (read), CreateChannel (read_write), JoinChannel (read_write). The users variable has read requirement from parent but no child requires read - this is acceptable as the parent's read requirement is satisfied by the parent's own access pattern (not delegated to children). All children's global_vars are subsets of parent's global_vars.
